violations.text: The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (0) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (200-400) ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment (can opener and slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed can opener and slicer in 3 bay sink for proper cleaning during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024
The water pressure of the hand sink in the back kitchen prep area was insufficient. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2024
The ceiling tiles, walls, and vents in the back storage and prep areas had an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the front service line. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice and took machine out of service to have cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Several foods (see disposal form) were observed held at 48-54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of all food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

All prepared items in the walk-in cooler were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

1) Establishment was not keeping daily records for monitoring indivdual food items meant to be held under Time as a Public Health Control 2) Establishment was not utilizing 'timers' as stated in their approved written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings observed in the warewashing and back storage areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

A water bottle and mugs were observed inside of a container of dry ingredients. Food must be protected from miscellaneous sources of contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The following utensils were observed improperly stored or used between uses: 1) Ice scoop was stored with handle in direct contact with ice 2) Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The front pizza prep unit was observed in poor repair (not holding adequate ambient temperature). PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat and/or chlorine sanitizing solution for the three bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris:1) All shelving units in walk-in cooler had large amount of biofilm 2) Fans covers in walk-in cooler had dust build up3) Large storage container had food residue and other debris 4) Storage racks in ware-washing area had rodent droppings, pooling water, and/or food resiude New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
The water pressure for both hot and cold water in the back hand sink was low. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Stuffed grape leaves and cut fruit were observed held at 50-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, Food was moved to walk-in. (PIC states grape leaves are kept in walk-in prior to opening) Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

Various foods were observed held at 43-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was prepped 2 hours ago and placed directly into unit. Food was moved to walk-in to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Food was prepped and placed directly into the cold hold display unit. Prepared food should be cooled fully to 41 degrees or less in a unit designed to be capable of cooling, such as a walk-in. PIC stated the new procedure would be to cool prepared foods in the walk-in prior to placing in the units up front. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Cake/pie packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Per PIC, they are waiting for new shipment of product to come in to use those labels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Inspector demonstrated how to properly sanitize. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Various foods in the cold hold unit were observed 58-65. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Establishment to store food items in deeper containers so that the containers are touching the cold well. Establishment to monitor food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
Establishment is using time for pizza without using logs. Inspector emailed logs for pizza. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Cake/pie packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
